intro: The Moabites were the people descended from Moab, the son of Lot (Gen. 19:37), who inhabited the plateau east of the Dead Sea between the Arnon and Zered rivers. From their original territory they expanded northward over land formerly held by the Amorites. Moabite religion centered on the national deity Chemosh, to whom human sacrifices were made (2 Kings 3:27). The Mosaic law excluded Moabites from the assembly of Israel to the tenth generation because of their hostility during the Exodus and their hiring of Balaam to curse Israel (Deut. 23:3–4; Neh. 13:1). Despite this exclusion, Ruth the Moabitess became the great-grandmother of David through her loyalty to Naomi and her confession of Israel's God (Ruth 1:16), and is listed in the genealogy of Jesus (Matt. 1:5). The prophets frequently addressed oracles against Moab (Isa. 15–16; Jer. 48; Amos 2:1–3), yet also held out the prospect of Moab's ultimate humbling and partial restoration.
